issues.display_title: Issue #13: Update Availability Open/Close Indicator & Handle Portfolio Name Hidden at No Preview Image
issues.main_description_html:
2026-06-19-availability-open-close-and-portfolio-name-overlay.md

Feature 1: Service Hours → Open / Closed Indicator

Context

The "Availability" section on the photographer profile page lists all days of the week with either a time range ("09:00 – 18:00") or "Closed". The API only returns which days the photographer is available — no actual time ranges are stored. The time string is a hardcoded display value.

  • AC1: The "Availability / Service hours" section never displays any time string (09:00, 18:00, 17:00, or any HH:MM format).
  • AC2: Every available day renders a green dot (bg-green-500) followed by the text "Open" in normal foreground color.
  • AC3: Every unavailable day renders a muted gray dot (bg-muted-foreground/40) followed by the text "Closed" in muted color (text-muted-foreground/60).
  • AC4: Both code paths produce the correct output — photographers who have availability data from the API, and those who fall back to the static default list.

Feature 2: Portfolio Thumbnail — Always Show Name Overlay

Context

Portfolio items in the 360 portfolio grid (photographerPortfolio.tsx) show a gradient overlay with the item name at the bottom of the thumbnail image. When no thumbnail is available (or image fails to load), a "No preview" placeholder is shown — but the item name is not displayed in this state.

  • AC1: Portfolio items with a valid thumbnail continue to show the gradient name overlay at the bottom, unchanged.
  • AC2: Portfolio items with no thumbnail (dashed border + camera icon + "No preview") also show the gradient name overlay at the bottom of the card.
  • AC3: If an item has neither businessName nor title, no empty gradient bar is rendered.
  • AC4: The behavior is consistent across all four portfolio types: GoThru, Google Street View, Panorra, and Matterport.

Demo by Developer
